content:
Another important symbol is the thorn bush. When Phoenix gets tangled in the bush and says, “It was the bush, and the bush was all over her,” it represents the challenges Phoenix faces in life, both physically and socially. Despite the pain, she keeps going and pushes through the obstacles to take care of what she needs to. The thorn bush symbolizes her strength and determination to reach her goal, no matter what stands in her way.

Overall, Eudora Welty’s “A Worn Path” uses symbolism to explore the themes of persistence and sacrifice. The worn path and thorn bush are powerful symbols of the emotional and physical struggles Phoenix faces. Her journey shows how far a loved one will go for someone they care about, making the story a reminder of the sacrifices people are willing to make for those they love.
